questions.stem: Under ASC 815, which of the following characteristics must a financial instrument possess to be classified as a derivative?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It must be exchange-traded

choices.label: B
choices.text: It has an underlying and a notional amount, requires no or small initial net investment, and can be net settled

choices.label: C
choices.text: It must have a maturity of more than one year

choices.label: D
choices.text: It must be denominated in a foreign currency

questions.explanation: ASC 815-10-15-83 defines a derivative as an instrument that: (1) has one or more underlyings and one or more notional amounts, (2) requires no initial net investment or a small one relative to other contracts with similar responses to market factors, and (3) its terms require or permit net settlement. Exchange-trading is not required.
questions.explanationJa: デリバティブの定義要件は3つ：(1) 1つ以上の基礎数値（underlying）と想定元本を有する、(2) 当初純投資が不要または少額、(3) 差金決済が可能。取引所取引であることは要件ではない。

questions.stem: A company designates a derivative as a fair value hedge of a recognized asset. Under ASC 815, how are gains and losses on the hedging instrument reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In other comprehensive income (OCI)

choices.label: B
choices.text: In earnings, along with the offsetting gain/loss on the hedged item attributable to the hedged risk

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred until the hedged item is sold

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an adjustment to the hedging instrument's notional amount

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-25-35-1, for a fair value hedge, both the gain/loss on the hedging derivative and the offsetting loss/gain on the hedged item (attributable to the hedged risk) are recognized in current earnings. This provides offset in the income statement, reducing earnings volatility from the hedged risk.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値ヘッジでは、ヘッジ手段（デリバティブ）の損益とヘッジ対象のヘッジリスクに帰属する相殺損益の両方を当期損益に認識する。損益計算書上で相殺が生じ、ヘッジリスクによる収益変動が軽減される。

questions.stem: On January 1, a company enters into an interest rate swap designated as a cash flow hedge of variable-rate debt. At December 31, the swap has a fair value gain of $80,000. The hedge is 100% effective. What amount is reported in other comprehensive income (OCI)?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-35-3, for a perfectly effective cash flow hedge, the entire gain or loss on the hedging derivative is initially reported in OCI (accumulated other comprehensive income). It is reclassified from AOCI to earnings in the period(s) when the hedged forecasted transaction affects earnings. Since the hedge is 100% effective, the full $80,000 goes to OCI.
questions.explanationJa: 完全に有効なキャッシュフローヘッジでは、ヘッジ手段の損益全額をまずOCIに計上する。ヘッジ対象の予定取引が損益に影響する期間にAOCIから損益へ振り替える。本問ではヘッジが100%有効なので、$80,000全額がOCIに計上される。

questions.stem: A company has a cash flow hedge. The cumulative change in the hedging derivative's fair value is a $120,000 gain. The cumulative change in the present value of expected cash flows on the hedged item is a $100,000 loss. What is reported in earnings for hedge ineffectiveness?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-35-3, the effective portion of a cash flow hedge ($100,000) is recorded in OCI. The ineffective portion (the excess of derivative gain over hedged item change) = $120,000 − $100,000 = $20,000 is recognized immediately in earnings. Only over-hedging creates an immediate earnings charge; under-hedging has no ineffectiveness recognized.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュフローヘッジの有効部分（$100,000）はOCIに計上する。非有効部分 = デリバティブ利益の超過額 = $120,000 - $100,000 = $20,000は即時に損益認識する。オーバーヘッジのみ即時損益計上となり、アンダーヘッジでは非有効部分を認識しない。

questions.stem: A company issues a $10,000,000 fixed-rate bond and enters into a receive-fixed/pay-variable interest rate swap to hedge the fair value risk. At year-end, interest rates have risen, causing the bond's fair value to decrease by $250,000 and the swap's fair value to increase by $240,000. What is the net effect on earnings from this hedge?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $10,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $240,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $250,000

questions.explanation: In a fair value hedge, both the derivative gain/loss and the hedged item's offsetting loss/gain are in earnings. Swap gain = $240,000; Bond fair value loss (attributable to hedged risk) = $250,000. Net earnings effect = $240,000 gain − $250,000 loss = $10,000 net loss. This $10,000 represents hedge ineffectiveness.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値ヘッジでは、デリバティブの損益とヘッジ対象の相殺損益の両方を損益計上する。スワップ利益 = $240,000、社債公正価値損失（ヘッジリスク帰属）= $250,000。損益への純影響 = $240,000 - $250,000 = -$10,000（純損失）。この$10,000がヘッジ非有効部分である。

questions.stem: A company purchases a call option on 10,000 shares at a strike price of $50 for a premium of $30,000. It does not designate the option as a hedge. At year-end, the option has a fair value of $45,000. What is the effect on Year 1 earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $15,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No effect until the option is exercised

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $45,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-10-35-2, derivatives not designated as hedging instruments are measured at fair value with changes in fair value recognized in current earnings. The option was purchased for $30,000 and its fair value at year-end is $45,000. Gain = $45,000 − $30,000 = $15,000, recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: ヘッジ指定のないデリバティブは公正価値で測定し、変動額を当期損益に認識する。オプション購入額$30,000、年末公正価値$45,000。利益 = $45,000 - $30,000 = $15,000を損益計上する。

questions.stem: A U.S. parent company designates a forward contract as a hedge of its net investment in a foreign subsidiary. The forward contract has a gain of $150,000 at year-end. Where is this gain reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In earnings as foreign exchange gain

choices.label: B
choices.text: In the cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) within OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an adjustment to goodwill

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a reduction of the investment in subsidiary

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-35-35-1, for a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation, the effective portion of the hedging instrument's gain or loss is reported in the cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) component of other comprehensive income, the same location as the translation gain/loss on the net investment. It remains in AOCI until the subsidiary is sold or liquidated.
questions.explanationJa: 在外事業体への純投資ヘッジでは、ヘッジ手段の有効部分の損益をOCIの為替換算調整勘定（CTA）に計上する。これは純投資自体の換算損益と同じ区分である。子会社の売却・清算時までAOCIに留まる。

questions.stem: A company issues a bond with a conversion feature that allows the holder to convert into common stock. Under ASC 815-15, when must the conversion feature be bifurcated and accounted for separately as a derivative?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Always, for all convertible bonds

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only when the embedded derivative is not clearly and closely related to the host contract and the host is not measured at fair value through earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Never; conversion features are always part of the host

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only if the bond is issued at a discount

questions.explanation: ASC 815-15-25-1 requires bifurcation of an embedded derivative when: (1) the economic characteristics of the embedded derivative are not clearly and closely related to the host contract, (2) the hybrid instrument is not remeasured at fair value with changes in fair value reported in earnings, and (3) a separate instrument with the same terms would meet the definition of a derivative. An equity conversion feature in an equity-host is clearly and closely related and thus not bifurcated.
questions.explanationJa: 組込デリバティブの分離（bifurcation）が必要な3条件：(1) 組込部分の経済的特性がホスト契約と明確かつ密接に関連しない、(2) 混合商品が公正価値で再測定されていない、(3) 同条件の独立商品がデリバティブの定義を満たす。株式ホストにおける株式転換条項はホストと密接に関連するため分離不要である。

questions.stem: A company used a cash flow hedge to hedge a forecasted inventory purchase. $60,000 of gain was accumulated in AOCI related to this hedge. The inventory is purchased and subsequently sold to customers. When is the $60,000 reclassified from AOCI to earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the inventory is purchased

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the inventory is sold (affects cost of goods sold)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ratably over the inventory holding period

choices.label: D
choices.text: Immediately when the hedge is discontinued

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-35-38, amounts in AOCI are reclassified to earnings in the period(s) the hedged forecasted transaction affects earnings. For a hedged inventory purchase, the AOCI amount adjusts the basis of the inventory and flows to cost of goods sold when the inventory is sold—that is when it affects earnings.
questions.explanationJa: AOCIの金額はヘッジ対象の予定取引が損益に影響する期間に振り替える。在庫購入のヘッジでは、AOCI累計額が在庫の取得原価を調整し、在庫販売時に売上原価として損益に反映される。

questions.stem: On October 1, a U.S. company enters a 90-day forward contract to sell €500,000 at a forward rate of $1.12/€. On December 31, the spot rate is $1.10/€ and the fair value of the forward contract (for the remaining 30 days) is determined to be a gain of $9,800. If the forward is not designated as a hedge, what is the journal entry at December 31?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Forward Contract Asset $9,800; Credit Foreign Exchange Gain $9,800

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Foreign Exchange Loss $9,800; Credit Forward Contract Liability $9,800

choices.label: C
choices.text: No entry until settlement

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Forward Contract Asset $10,000; Credit Foreign Exchange Gain $10,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-10-35-2, derivatives not designated as hedges are marked to fair value at each reporting date with gains/losses in earnings. The forward contract to sell euros has gained value because the euro weakened (spot $1.10 < forward $1.12), meaning the company will sell at a rate above market. Fair value gain of $9,800 is recognized: Dr Forward Contract Asset, Cr Foreign Exchange Gain.
questions.explanationJa: ヘッジ指定のないデリバティブは各報告日に公正価値で評価し損益に計上する。ユーロ売りの先渡契約は、ユーロ下落（スポット$1.10 < 先渡レート$1.12）により価値が上昇した。公正価値利益$9,800を認識する。仕訳：借方 先渡契約資産 $9,800 / 貸方 為替差益 $9,800。

questions.subtopic: Interest Rate Swap - Fair Value Hedge
questions.stem: A company holds a fixed-rate bond investment with a carrying value of $1,000,000 and designates a pay-fixed/receive-variable interest rate swap as a fair value hedge. At year-end, the swap has a fair value loss of $35,000 and the bond's fair value increased by $33,000 due to the hedged risk. What is the net effect on earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $2,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $35,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $33,000

questions.explanation: In a fair value hedge, both the derivative's gain/loss and the hedged item's offsetting gain/loss are recognized in earnings. Swap loss = $35,000; Bond gain (hedged risk) = $33,000. Net = −$35,000 + $33,000 = −$2,000 loss. The $2,000 represents hedge ineffectiveness.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値ヘッジでは、デリバティブの損益$-35,000とヘッジ対象の損益$+33,000の両方を損益に認識する。純影響 = -$35,000 + $33,000 = -$2,000の損失。$2,000がヘッジ非有効部分。

questions.stem: A company designates a derivative as a cash flow hedge of a highly probable forecasted purchase of raw materials expected in 6 months. What condition must be met for the forecasted transaction to qualify for hedge accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The forecasted transaction must be probable of occurring

choices.label: B
choices.text: The forecasted transaction must have already been contractually committed

choices.label: C
choices.text: The transaction must occur within 30 days of designation

choices.label: D
choices.text: The transaction must be with a related party

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-20-25-15(b), a forecasted transaction must be probable of occurring to qualify as a hedged item in a cash flow hedge. It does not need to be a firm commitment—a firm commitment would qualify for a fair value hedge instead. The assessment considers the entity's history and business practices.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュフローヘッジの対象となるには、予定取引が発生する蓋然性が高い（probable）ことが要件。確定約定（firm commitment）である必要はない。確定約定は公正価値ヘッジの対象となる。

questions.stem: Under ASC 815 (as amended by ASU 2017-12), which of the following is true regarding hedge effectiveness assessment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Entities may perform only a qualitative assessment of hedge effectiveness if certain conditions are met

choices.label: B
choices.text: A quantitative assessment using the dollar-offset method is always required every quarter

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hedge effectiveness testing was eliminated entirely by ASU 2017-12

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only retrospective testing is required; prospective testing was eliminated

questions.explanation: ASU 2017-12 simplified hedge effectiveness requirements. Entities may elect to perform only a qualitative (rather than quantitative) assessment of hedge effectiveness after initial quantitative testing, if they can reasonably support that the hedge relationship will be highly effective. The 80-125% bright-line test was eliminated.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-12はヘッジ有効性の要件を簡素化した。当初の定量テスト後、ヘッジ関係が高度に有効であると合理的に裏付けられる場合、定性的な評価のみを選択できる。80-125%のブライトライン基準は廃止された。

questions.stem: A company purchases a put option for $20,000 to hedge the fair value of an inventory position. The company elects to exclude the option's time value from the hedge effectiveness assessment. At year-end, the option's intrinsic value increased by $15,000 and the time value decreased by $8,000. How is the $8,000 time value decline reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Amortized to earnings on a systematic and rational basis over the hedge period

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized immediately in earnings as hedge ineffectiveness

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognized in OCI permanently

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ignored and not recorded

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-20-25-83A (as amended by ASU 2017-12), when an entity excludes time value from the assessment of effectiveness, the initial value of the excluded component may be amortized to earnings on a systematic and rational basis over the life of the hedging instrument. Changes in fair value of the excluded component are recorded in OCI initially.
questions.explanationJa: オプションの時間価値をヘッジ有効性評価から除外する場合、除外部分の当初価値をヘッジ手段の存続期間にわたり体系的・合理的にに損益に償却できる。除外部分の公正価値変動は当初OCIに計上する。

questions.stem: A U.S. company enters into a cross-currency interest rate swap to hedge the foreign currency risk of a euro-denominated fixed-rate bond payable. The swap is designated as a cash flow hedge. At year-end, the effective portion of the swap gain is $45,000. Where is this gain reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In OCI (AOCI), to be reclassified to earnings when the hedged interest payments affect earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: In current earnings as interest expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a reduction to the carrying value of the bond

choices.label: D
choices.text: In the cumulative translation adjustment

questions.explanation: For a cash flow hedge, the effective portion of the hedging instrument's gain/loss is reported in OCI. It is reclassified from AOCI to earnings in the period(s) the hedged forecasted transactions (interest payments) affect earnings. A cross-currency swap hedging foreign currency risk on debt is treated as a cash flow hedge.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュフローヘッジの有効部分はOCIに計上し、ヘッジ対象の予定取引（利払い）が損益に影響する期間にAOCIから損益へ振り替える。外貨建て負債の為替リスクを通貨スワップでヘッジする場合、キャッシュフローヘッジとして処理される。

questions.stem: A company discontinues a cash flow hedge because the forecasted transaction is no longer probable of occurring. At the time of discontinuation, $50,000 of gain is in AOCI. How should this amount be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassified immediately from AOCI to earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remain in AOCI until the original forecasted date

choices.label: C
choices.text: Charged to equity permanently

choices.label: D
choices.text: Returned to the counterparty

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-40-4, if a hedged forecasted transaction is no longer probable of occurring, amounts accumulated in AOCI must be reclassified immediately to earnings. If the hedge is discontinued but the forecasted transaction is still probable, AOCI amounts remain until the transaction occurs.
questions.explanationJa: ヘッジ対象の予定取引が発生する蓋然性が低下した場合、AOCIの累積額を即時に損益へ振り替える。予定取引がなお蓋然性を保つ場合はAOCIに残し、取引発生時に振り替える。

questions.stem: Which of the following contracts is specifically excluded from the scope of ASC 815 (Derivatives and Hedging)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Regular-way security trades (normal purchases and sales with delivery within the standard settlement period)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Interest rate swaps on variable-rate debt

choices.label: C
choices.text: Foreign currency forward contracts

choices.label: D
choices.text: Commodity futures traded on an exchange

questions.explanation: ASC 815-10-15-141 provides a scope exception for regular-way security trades—contracts to purchase or sell securities that require delivery within the period generally established by regulations or conventions in the marketplace. Interest rate swaps, foreign currency forwards, and commodity futures are within scope.
questions.explanationJa: 通常の有価証券売買取引（市場の規制や慣行で定められた決済期間内に引渡しが行われる取引）はASC 815の適用範囲から除外される。金利スワップ、為替先渡、商品先物は適用範囲内。

questions.stem: A coffee roaster designates a futures contract as a fair value hedge of 100,000 pounds of coffee inventory. At year-end, the futures gain is $18,000 and the inventory fair value decreased by $17,500 due to the hedged commodity price risk. What is the carrying value adjustment to the inventory?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $17,500

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase of $18,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No adjustment; the inventory remains at cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease of $500

questions.explanation: In a fair value hedge of inventory, the hedged item's carrying value is adjusted for the gain or loss attributable to the hedged risk. The inventory carrying value decreases by $17,500 (the loss on the hedged item). Both amounts go to earnings: Futures gain $18,000 and Inventory loss $17,500, resulting in net earnings of $500.
questions.explanationJa: 在庫の公正価値ヘッジでは、ヘッジリスクに帰属する損益分だけ在庫の帳簿価額を調整する。在庫は$17,500減少し、先物利益$18,000と在庫損失$17,500の両方が損益に計上される。純影響は$500の利益。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2017-12, a company designates a cash flow hedge of a forecasted purchase of equipment. Upon purchase, $30,000 of gain is accumulated in AOCI. The company elects the basis adjustment approach. How does this affect the equipment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The cost basis of the equipment is reduced by $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: The $30,000 remains in AOCI and is amortized over the equipment's useful life

choices.label: C
choices.text: The $30,000 is immediately recognized in earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: The cost basis of the equipment is increased by $30,000

questions.explanation: Under the basis adjustment approach permitted by ASU 2017-12, AOCI amounts from a cash flow hedge of a forecasted purchase of a nonfinancial asset are reclassified from AOCI to adjust the cost basis of the asset when it is recognized. A $30,000 gain reduces the asset's initial cost, which then affects future depreciation.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-12で認められた基礎調整アプローチでは、非金融資産の予定購入に対するキャッシュフローヘッジのAOCI累積額を、資産認識時に取得原価の調整として振り替える。$30,000の利益は設備の取得原価を減少させ、将来の減価償却に影響する。

questions.stem: At what amount should a derivative instrument be initially recognized on the balance sheet under ASC 815?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Notional amount

choices.label: C
choices.text: Intrinsic value only

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cost or amortized cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-10-30-1, all derivative instruments are recognized as either assets or liabilities at fair value on the balance sheet. This applies at initial recognition and at each subsequent reporting date. The notional amount is not the carrying value—it is used to calculate payments.
questions.explanationJa: デリバティブは当初認識時に公正価値で資産または負債として貸借対照表に計上する。これは当初認識時および以後の各報告日に適用される。想定元本は帳簿価額ではなく、支払計算に使用される。

questions.stem: A company issues a bond denominated in a foreign currency with a principal-indexed provision that adjusts the repayment amount based on a commodity price index. The commodity index is not clearly and closely related to the debt host. Which statement is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The commodity-indexed provision must be bifurcated and accounted for as a separate derivative

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entire bond is accounted for as a derivative

choices.label: C
choices.text: No bifurcation is needed because it is a debt instrument

choices.label: D
choices.text: The provision is recognized only at settlement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-15-25-1, an embedded derivative must be bifurcated when (1) it is not clearly and closely related to the host, (2) the hybrid is not measured at fair value through earnings, and (3) the embedded feature meets the derivative definition. A commodity index in a debt host is not clearly and closely related, so bifurcation is required.
questions.explanationJa: 商品価格連動条項は負債のホスト契約と明確かつ密接に関連しないため、分離が必要。混合商品が損益を通じて公正価値で測定されておらず、組込部分がデリバティブの定義を満たす場合に分離する。

questions.stem: Under a fair value hedge, the carrying value of the hedged item is adjusted for the change in fair value attributable to the hedged risk. If the hedged item is an AFS debt security, where is the hedge-related adjustment recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In earnings, not in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: In OCI, consistent with AFS treatment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Partially in earnings and partially in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a direct adjustment to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-25-35-1, for a fair value hedge, the gain or loss on the hedged item attributable to the hedged risk is recognized in earnings (not OCI), even if the hedged item is an AFS security that would otherwise report unrealized gains/losses in OCI. This ensures the offset with the derivative gain/loss occurs in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値ヘッジでは、ヘッジ対象がAFS証券であっても、ヘッジリスクに帰属する損益は損益計算書に認識する（OCIではない）。これにより、デリバティブの損益との相殺が損益計算書上で実現する。

questions.stem: Which of the following can be designated as a hedging instrument under ASC 815?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A derivative instrument such as a swap, forward, future, or purchased option

choices.label: B
choices.text: A nonderivative financial instrument such as a receivable

choices.label: C
choices.text: An entity's own equity shares held in treasury

choices.label: D
choices.text: A written option with no intrinsic value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-20-25, a hedging instrument must generally be a derivative. Nonderivative instruments may only serve as hedging instruments in limited circumstances (e.g., foreign currency hedges of net investments). Written options alone generally cannot be hedging instruments because they provide limited offset potential.
questions.explanationJa: ヘッジ手段は原則としてデリバティブでなければならない。非デリバティブは限定的な場合（例：純投資の外貨ヘッジ）のみ使用可能。売建オプション単独は相殺能力が限定的なためヘッジ手段として認められない。

questions.stem: A U.S. parent designates a €10,000,000 euro-denominated borrowing as a hedge of its net investment in a European subsidiary. During the year, the euro weakens, resulting in a $200,000 foreign exchange gain on the borrowing. The effective portion of the gain is $200,000. How is this reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $200,000 gain in the cumulative translation adjustment within OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: $200,000 gain in earnings as foreign exchange gain

choices.label: C
choices.text: $200,000 gain as a reduction of the investment in the subsidiary

choices.label: D
choices.text: $200,000 gain deferred until the subsidiary is sold

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-35-35-1, for a net investment hedge, the effective portion of the hedging instrument's gain/loss is recorded in the CTA component of OCI. For a nonderivative hedging instrument (like foreign-currency debt), the entire foreign exchange gain/loss goes to CTA. It is reclassified to earnings only upon sale/liquidation of the subsidiary.
questions.explanationJa: 純投資ヘッジの有効部分は、OCIの累積換算調整勘定（CTA）に計上する。非デリバティブのヘッジ手段（外貨建負債など）では、為替損益全額がCTAに計上される。子会社の売却・清算時にのみ損益へ振り替える。

questions.stem: A company enters into a 6-month forward contract to purchase British pounds at a forward rate of $1.28/£ when the spot rate is $1.25/£. The $0.03 difference represents the forward premium. If designated as a cash flow hedge with the forward points excluded, how are the forward points accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Amortized to earnings ratably over the 6-month life or recognized in OCI with changes in fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized entirely in earnings at inception

choices.label: C
choices.text: Capitalized as part of the hedged item's cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ignored and not recorded

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-20-25-82A (ASU 2017-12), when forward points are excluded from hedge effectiveness assessment, the entity may elect to amortize the initial value of the excluded component (the forward points) to earnings on a systematic basis, or recognize changes in the excluded component's fair value in earnings through OCI initially.
questions.explanationJa: 先渡ポイント（プレミアム/ディスカウント）をヘッジ有効性評価から除外する場合、除外部分の当初価値を体系的に損益に償却するか、公正価値変動をまずOCIに認識してから損益に計上する方法を選択できる。

questions.stem: A company has a cash flow hedge of a forecasted export sale expected in 3 months. Two months into the hedge, the probability of the transaction drops from 'probable' to 'reasonably possible.' What accounting action is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The hedge must be de-designated, but AOCI amounts remain until it is probable the transaction will not occur

choices.label: B
choices.text: The hedge continues with no change in accounting

choices.label: C
choices.text: AOCI amounts are immediately reclassified to earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: The derivative must be settled immediately

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-40-3, if the forecasted transaction is no longer probable but is still reasonably possible, the hedge is de-designated prospectively. However, amounts already in AOCI remain there and are not reclassified to earnings until it is probable the transaction will not occur within the originally specified time period.
questions.explanationJa: 予定取引の蓋然性が「probable」から「reasonably possible」に低下した場合、ヘッジ指定を将来に向けて解除する。ただし、AOCIの累積額は取引が当初の時期に発生しないことがprobableになるまで残置し、その時点で損益へ振り替える。

questions.stem: A company holds an interest rate swap with a notional amount of $5,000,000. The swap has no quoted market price. How should the company determine the fair value of the swap?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Using a valuation model based on the present value of expected future net cash flows (Level 2 or Level 3 inputs)

choices.label: B
choices.text: At the notional amount of the swap

choices.label: C
choices.text: At zero, since swaps have no initial investment

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the cumulative net cash settled to date

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820, derivatives without quoted prices are valued using valuation techniques, typically discounted cash flow models using observable market inputs (Level 2) such as yield curves and credit spreads. The notional amount is not the fair value—it is only used to calculate cash flow exchanges.
questions.explanationJa: 市場価格がないデリバティブは、利回り曲線や信用スプレッドなどの観察可能な市場データ（レベル2）を用いた割引キャッシュフロー法等で公正価値を算定する。想定元本は公正価値ではなく、キャッシュフロー交換の計算にのみ使用される。

questions.subtopic: Hedge of Foreign Currency Firm Commitment
questions.stem: A U.S. company enters a firm commitment to purchase equipment from a German supplier for €2,000,000 in 6 months. The company designates a forward contract as a fair value hedge of the foreign currency risk. At year-end, the forward contract has a gain of $40,000 and the firm commitment has a loss of $40,000. What is recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A firm commitment asset/liability of $40,000 and derivative asset of $40,000, with offsetting gains/losses in earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the derivative gain of $40,000 in earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both amounts in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry until settlement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-25-35-9, in a fair value hedge of a foreign currency firm commitment, the firm commitment is recognized as an asset or liability for the change attributable to the hedged risk. Both the derivative gain ($40,000) and firm commitment loss ($40,000) are recognized in earnings, offsetting each other.
questions.explanationJa: 外貨建確定約定の公正価値ヘッジでは、確定約定をヘッジリスクの変動額として資産/負債に認識する。デリバティブ利益$40,000と確定約定損失$40,000の両方を損益に認識し、相殺される。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2022-01, a bank designates a portfolio layer method hedge for a closed portfolio of fixed-rate loans totaling $100,000,000. The designated hedged layer is the last $30,000,000. What is the primary benefit of this method?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It allows the entity to hedge a stated amount of the portfolio without being impacted by prepayments up to $70,000,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: It eliminates all hedge ineffectiveness

choices.label: C
choices.text: It allows hedging of variable-rate loans

choices.label: D
choices.text: It permits hedging without designation documentation

questions.explanation: Under the portfolio layer method (ASU 2022-01, codified in ASC 815-20-25-12A), an entity can designate a hedged layer (e.g., last $30M of a $100M portfolio). Prepayments reduce the portfolio from the top, so the hedged bottom layer is protected from prepayment risk as long as the portfolio exceeds the layer amount.
questions.explanationJa: ポートフォリオレイヤー法では、$100Mのポートフォリオの最後の$30Mをヘッジ対象として指定できる。期限前返済はポートフォリオの上層から減少するため、ポートフォリオ残高がレイヤー額を超える限り、ヘッジ対象は期限前返済リスクから保護される。

questions.stem: Under ASC 815-10-50, which of the following disclosures is required for derivative instruments?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The fair value of derivative instruments, their gains and losses, and information about credit-risk-related contingent features

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the notional amounts of all derivatives

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the names of the counterparties

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclosures are required only for derivatives designated as hedges

questions.explanation: ASC 815-10-50 requires comprehensive disclosures including: fair values and balance sheet locations of derivatives (segregated between those designated as hedges and those not), gains and losses recognized, notional amounts, and information about credit-risk-related contingent features. These apply to all derivatives, not just those in hedging relationships.
questions.explanationJa: デリバティブの開示要件は包括的で、ヘッジ指定の有無に関係なくすべてのデリバティブに適用される。公正価値と貸借対照表上の位置、認識された損益、想定元本、信用リスク関連の偶発条件に関する情報の開示が求められる。

questions.stem: Under ASC 815, where must all derivative instruments be reported on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As either assets or liabilities measured at fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: As off-balance-sheet items disclosed in the notes only

choices.label: C
choices.text: At historical cost in other assets or other liabilities

choices.label: D
choices.text: At amortized cost within the related hedged item line

questions.explanation: ASC 815-10-35-1 requires that all derivative instruments be recognized on the balance sheet as either assets or liabilities and measured at fair value. There is no off-balance-sheet treatment permitted for derivatives under current GAAP. The fair value measurement applies regardless of whether the derivative is designated as a hedging instrument.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815に基づき、すべてのデリバティブは貸借対照表上で資産または負債として公正価値で認識しなければならない。オフバランス処理は認められていない。

questions.stem: Reed Corp. designates an interest rate swap as a fair value hedge of its fixed-rate bond payable. During the period, the swap gains $50,000 in fair value while the bond's fair value decreases by $48,000 due to interest rate changes. What amount of hedge ineffectiveness is recognized in earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $2,000 gain

questions.explanation: In a fair value hedge, both the change in fair value of the hedging instrument ($50,000 gain) and the change in fair value of the hedged item attributable to the hedged risk ($48,000 loss) are recognized in earnings. The net effect of $2,000 ($50,000 − $48,000) represents the hedge ineffectiveness recognized in current period earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値ヘッジでは、ヘッジ手段の公正価値変動（$50,000利益）とヘッジ対象の公正価値変動（$48,000損失）の両方が損益計上される。差額$2,000がヘッジ非有効部分として認識される。

questions.stem: Kline Inc. uses a cash flow hedge for a forecasted inventory purchase. The derivative accumulates $30,000 in OCI. When should Kline reclassify this amount from AOCI into earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the inventory is sold to a third party

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the forecasted purchase occurs

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the end of each reporting period on a pro-rata basis

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the hedging relationship is terminated

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-35-38, for a cash flow hedge of a forecasted purchase of a nonfinancial asset (such as inventory), the amounts in AOCI are reclassified into earnings in the same period(s) that the hedged item affects earnings. Since inventory affects earnings when it is sold (as cost of goods sold), the reclassification occurs when the inventory is sold to a third party, not when it is purchased.
questions.explanationJa: 棚卸資産の予定購入に対するキャッシュ・フロー・ヘッジでは、AOCIに計上された金額は、ヘッジ対象が損益に影響する期間（棚卸資産が第三者に販売された時点）に損益に振り替えられる。

questions.stem: Which of the following conditions must ALL be met to require bifurcation of an embedded derivative from its host contract under ASC 815?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The hybrid instrument is not remeasured at fair value through earnings, the embedded derivative meets the definition of a derivative, and the economic characteristics are not clearly and closely related to the host

choices.label: B
choices.text: The embedded derivative has a notional amount greater than $100,000 and the host contract is a debt instrument

choices.label: C
choices.text: The host contract is a financial instrument and the embedded derivative has a readily determinable fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: The embedded derivative causes the host contract to have a maturity of greater than one year

questions.explanation: ASC 815-15-25-1 requires bifurcation of an embedded derivative when three conditions are met: (1) the economic characteristics and risks of the embedded derivative are not clearly and closely related to the host contract, (2) the hybrid instrument is not remeasured at fair value with changes in fair value reported in earnings, and (3) a separate instrument with the same terms would be a derivative. All three conditions must be satisfied.
questions.explanationJa: 組込デリバティブの分離要件は3つ：①組込デリバティブの経済的特性がホスト契約と明確かつ密接に関連していない、②混合契約が公正価値で損益測定されていない、③同条件の独立した商品がデリバティブの定義を満たす。3つすべてを満たす必要がある。

questions.stem: Apex Corp. designates a foreign currency forward contract as a hedge of its net investment in a British subsidiary. The forward contract gains $120,000, while the translation loss on the net investment is $115,000. Under the spot method, where is the effective portion of the hedge gain reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In the cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: In current period earnings as a foreign currency gain

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a direct adjustment to retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a reduction of the investment in subsidiary

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-35-35-1, for a net investment hedge, the effective portion of the hedging instrument's gain or loss is reported in OCI as part of the cumulative translation adjustment (CTA). This parallels the treatment of the translation adjustment on the hedged net investment. Any ineffective portion would be recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 純投資ヘッジでは、ヘッジ手段の有効部分の損益はOCIの累積換算調整額（CTA）に計上される。これはヘッジ対象である純投資の換算調整と同じ処理となる。非有効部分は損益に認識される。

questions.stem: Bergen Co. voluntarily discontinues a cash flow hedge designation. At the date of discontinuation, $40,000 of gains remain in AOCI. The original forecasted transaction is still probable of occurring. How should Bergen account for the $40,000?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retain the $40,000 in AOCI and reclassify it when the forecasted transaction affects earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Immediately reclassify the $40,000 from AOCI into earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reverse the $40,000 against the derivative's carrying amount

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amortize the $40,000 over the remaining life of the derivative

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-40-1, when a cash flow hedge is discontinued but the forecasted transaction is still probable, the net gain or loss in AOCI remains there and is reclassified into earnings when the forecasted transaction affects earnings. Immediate reclassification into earnings would only occur if the forecasted transaction is no longer probable of occurring.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュ・フロー・ヘッジの指定を中止しても、予定取引がなお発生見込みの場合、AOCIの累積損益はそのまま残り、予定取引が損益に影響する時点で振り替える。予定取引の発生が見込めなくなった場合にのみ即時損益認識される。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Patel Corp. enters into a 3-year pay-fixed, receive-variable interest rate swap with a notional amount of $5,000,000. The fixed rate is 4% and the variable rate resets quarterly to SOFR. At inception, what is the fair value of this swap?

choices.label: C
choices.text: $200,000 liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cannot be determined without the current SOFR rate

questions.explanation: At inception, a plain-vanilla interest rate swap is structured so that the fixed rate equals the market rate, making the present value of expected fixed payments equal to the present value of expected variable payments. Therefore, the fair value at inception is zero. The swap will develop a positive or negative fair value as market interest rates change subsequent to inception.
questions.explanationJa: 金利スワップは開始時点で固定金利が市場金利と等しく設定されるため、固定支払の現在価値と変動支払の期待現在価値が等しくなり、公正価値はゼロとなる。その後の金利変動により正または負の公正価値が生じる。

questions.subtopic: Cash Flow Hedge Forecasted Transaction Probability
questions.stem: Which of the following is the probability threshold for a forecasted transaction to qualify as the hedged item in a cash flow hedge under ASC 815?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Probable (likely to occur)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reasonably possible

choices.label: C
choices.text: More likely than not (greater than 50%)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Virtually certain

questions.explanation: ASC 815-20-25-15(b) requires that a forecasted transaction be probable of occurring to be designated as the hedged item in a cash flow hedge. Under U.S. GAAP, 'probable' is defined as 'likely to occur,' which is a higher threshold than 'more likely than not' (>50%) but lower than 'virtually certain.' The entity must have the ability and likelihood to complete the transaction.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュ・フロー・ヘッジのヘッジ対象となる予定取引は「probable（発生の可能性が高い）」でなければならない。これは「more likely than not（50%超）」より高い基準だが「virtually certain」より低い基準である。

questions.subtopic: Fair Value Hedge of Available-for-Sale Debt
questions.stem: Troy Inc. holds an available-for-sale (AFS) debt security and designates an interest rate swap as a fair value hedge of the security's interest rate risk. During the period, the swap's fair value increases by $25,000 and the AFS security's fair value decreases by $24,000 due to interest rate changes. Where are these changes reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both the $25,000 gain and $24,000 loss are reported in earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: The $25,000 gain is in earnings; the $24,000 loss is in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both are reported in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: The net $1,000 gain is reported in earnings; the remainder is in OCI

questions.explanation: In a fair value hedge, the change in fair value of the hedging instrument and the change in fair value of the hedged item attributable to the hedged risk are both recognized in earnings (ASC 815-25-35-1). Even though the hedged item is an AFS security (which would normally have fair value changes in OCI), the fair value hedge designation requires the hedged risk portion to be reported in earnings, overriding the normal AFS accounting.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値ヘッジでは、ヘッジ手段とヘッジ対象（ヘッジリスクに起因する部分）の公正価値変動はともに損益に認識される。AFS証券は通常OCIで処理されるが、公正価値ヘッジ指定により損益計上に変更される。

questions.stem: Vance Corp. purchases a put option for $8,000 to hedge a forecasted sale of inventory and elects to exclude the option's time value from the hedge assessment. At the hedge inception, the intrinsic value of the option is $0 and the time value is $8,000. How should Vance account for the $8,000 time value?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Amortize it on a systematic and rational basis over the hedge period, recognized in earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record it in OCI along with the intrinsic value changes

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense the entire $8,000 immediately at inception

choices.label: D
choices.text: Capitalize it as part of the cost basis of the hedged inventory

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-20-25-83A, when an entity elects to exclude the time value of an option from the hedge effectiveness assessment for a cash flow hedge of a forecasted transaction, the excluded component's initial value is amortized using a systematic and rational method over the life of the hedging instrument. The amortization is recognized in earnings. This is the 'amortization approach' for excluded components.
questions.explanationJa: オプションの時間価値をヘッジ有効性評価から除外する場合、ASC 815-20-25-83Aに基づき、除外要素の当初価値はヘッジ期間にわたり合理的な方法で償却し損益に認識する（償却アプローチ）。

questions.stem: Harris Co. enters into a commodity futures contract for speculative purposes (no hedge designation). At year-end, the futures contract has an unrealized gain of $35,000. How should Harris report this gain?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize the $35,000 gain in current period earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Defer the gain in OCI until the contract is settled

choices.label: C
choices.text: No recognition until the contract is settled

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize in earnings only if the gain exceeds the initial margin

questions.explanation: Derivatives not designated as hedging instruments are measured at fair value with all changes in fair value recognized in current period earnings (ASC 815-10-35-2). There is no deferral mechanism available for derivatives held for speculative or trading purposes. The gain is reported in earnings regardless of whether the position has been closed.
questions.explanationJa: ヘッジ指定されていないデリバティブは公正価値で測定され、すべての公正価値変動が当期損益に認識される。投機目的のデリバティブには繰延処理は適用されない。

questions.stem: At the inception of a hedging relationship, which of the following is NOT required to be formally documented under ASC 815?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The exact dollar amount of expected ineffectiveness

choices.label: B
choices.text: The risk management objective and strategy for undertaking the hedge

choices.label: C
choices.text: The method for assessing hedge effectiveness

choices.label: D
choices.text: Identification of the hedging instrument and the hedged item

questions.explanation: ASC 815-20-25-3 requires that at inception of a hedge, the entity formally document: the hedging relationship, the risk management objective and strategy, identification of the hedging instrument and hedged item or transaction, the nature of the risk being hedged, and the method for assessing effectiveness. The exact dollar amount of expected ineffectiveness is not required; rather, the entity must document how it will assess whether the hedge is expected to be highly effective.
questions.explanationJa: ヘッジ開始時の文書化要件には、ヘッジ関係、リスク管理目的と戦略、ヘッジ手段とヘッジ対象の識別、有効性評価方法が含まれる。予想される非有効部分の正確な金額は要求されていない。

questions.stem: Global Inc. has a yen-denominated fixed-rate debt and enters into a cross-currency interest rate swap to pay USD fixed and receive JPY fixed. This swap can be designated as a hedge of which of the following risks?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both the foreign currency risk and the interest rate risk of the yen-denominated debt

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the foreign currency risk of the yen-denominated debt

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the interest rate risk of the yen-denominated debt

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neither risk, because cross-currency swaps cannot be designated as hedging instruments

questions.explanation: A cross-currency interest rate swap can be designated as a fair value hedge or cash flow hedge of both the foreign currency risk and the interest rate risk embedded in foreign-currency-denominated debt. ASC 815-20-25-12 permits the hedging of multiple risks simultaneously when the hedging instrument is a cross-currency swap because it effectively converts both the currency and interest rate characteristics of the hedged item.
questions.explanationJa: 通貨金利スワップは、外貨建て債務の為替リスクと金利リスクの両方のヘッジ手段として指定できる。ASC 815-20-25-12により、通貨と金利の両方の特性を変換するため、複数リスクの同時ヘッジが認められている。

questions.stem: Which of the following contracts is specifically excluded from the scope of ASC 815 (Derivatives and Hedging)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A traditional life insurance contract

choices.label: B
choices.text: A commodity forward contract with net settlement

choices.label: C
choices.text: An interest rate cap purchased from a bank

choices.label: D
choices.text: A foreign currency option traded on an exchange

questions.explanation: ASC 815-10-15-13 provides several scope exceptions, including traditional insurance contracts (life, property, casualty). These contracts transfer insurance risk and are accounted for under insurance-specific guidance (ASC 944). Commodity forwards with net settlement, interest rate caps, and exchange-traded currency options all meet the derivative definition and fall within ASC 815's scope.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-10-15-13により、伝統的な保険契約（生命保険、損害保険等）はデリバティブの範囲から除外される。保険リスクを移転する契約はASC 944の保険会計基準に従う。

questions.stem: Dunn Corp. designates a forward contract as a cash flow hedge. During the period, the forward contract increases in value by $80,000, but the present value of the expected change in cash flows of the hedged forecasted transaction is only $70,000. What amount is recorded in OCI?
questions.explanation: Under the 'lower of' approach in ASC 815-30-35-3, the amount recorded in OCI for a cash flow hedge is limited to the lesser of (a) the cumulative gain or loss on the derivative from inception, or (b) the cumulative change in the present value of the expected future cash flows on the hedged transaction from inception. Here, OCI is limited to $70,000 (the lesser amount), and the remaining $10,000 is recognized in earnings as hedge ineffectiveness.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュ・フロー・ヘッジでは、OCIに計上される金額は「デリバティブの累積損益」と「ヘッジ対象の予想キャッシュ・フローの累積変動の現在価値」のいずれか小さい方に制限される。差額$10,000はヘッジ非有効部分として損益に認識される。

questions.stem: Under the last-of-layer method introduced by ASU 2017-12, an entity can designate which of the following as the hedged item in a fair value hedge?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A stated amount of the asset or liability that is not expected to be prepaid, representing the last layer

choices.label: B
choices.text: The first layer of a closed portfolio of prepayable financial assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: A single identified loan within a portfolio of similar loans

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entire notional amount of a portfolio regardless of prepayment risk

questions.explanation: The last-of-layer method (ASC 815-20-25-12A, introduced by ASU 2017-12) allows an entity to designate a stated amount of a closed portfolio of prepayable financial assets (or one or more beneficial interests in a securitization) as the hedged item, where that amount represents the 'last layer' that is not expected to be affected by prepayments. This reduces the risk of hedge ineffectiveness caused by prepayments.
questions.explanationJa: ラスト・オブ・レイヤー法では、期限前償還可能な金融資産のクローズド・ポートフォリオのうち、期限前償還の影響を受けないと見込まれる最後のレイヤー（一定金額）をヘッジ対象に指定できる。

questions.stem: Chen Corp. enters into a forward contract to purchase 10,000 bushels of wheat for physical delivery to use in its manufacturing process. Under what condition can Chen elect the normal purchases and normal sales (NPNS) scope exception under ASC 815?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The contract requires physical delivery, is for quantities expected to be used in the normal course of business, and there is no past practice of net settlement

choices.label: B
choices.text: The contract has a notional amount below $1 million

choices.label: C
choices.text: The contract is with a related party and involves no cash settlement

choices.label: D
choices.text: The contract matures within 30 days of the balance sheet date

questions.explanation: ASC 815-10-15-22 through 15-51 allows the NPNS scope exception for contracts that provide for physical delivery of a quantity expected to be used or sold in the normal course of business. The entity must not have a pattern of net settling similar contracts or taking delivery and shortly thereafter selling the commodity. The election must be documented, and once designated, the contract is not accounted for as a derivative.
questions.explanationJa: NPNS例外を適用するには、①物理的受渡しを要する契約、②通常業務で使用・販売する数量、③純額決済の慣行がない、の条件を満たす必要がある。この指定により、契約はデリバティブとして会計処理されない。

questions.stem: Parker Inc. has a firm commitment to purchase equipment from a German supplier for €500,000 in 6 months. Parker designates a forward contract to buy euros as a fair value hedge of the foreign currency risk. At year-end, the euro appreciates and the forward contract has a fair value gain of $18,000. How should Parker account for this hedge?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize the $18,000 derivative gain in earnings and record an offsetting $18,000 asset (or liability) for the firm commitment's change in fair value attributable to foreign currency risk

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the $18,000 gain in OCI until the equipment is purchased

choices.label: C
choices.text: Defer the gain as part of the cost basis of the equipment

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize the $18,000 gain in earnings with no offset until settlement

questions.explanation: A hedge of the foreign currency risk in a firm commitment is accounted for as a fair value hedge (ASC 815-25-35-9). Both the gain on the derivative ($18,000) and the loss on the firm commitment attributable to the hedged risk are recognized in earnings. An asset or liability is recognized for the cumulative change in fair value of the firm commitment attributable to the hedged risk. When the equipment is purchased, the firm commitment asset/liability adjusts the initial carrying amount of the equipment.
questions.explanationJa: 外貨建てファーム・コミットメントのヘッジは公正価値ヘッジとして処理される。デリバティブの利益$18,000とファーム・コミットメントの損失を共に損益認識し、ファーム・コミットメントの公正価値変動を資産/負債として計上する。設備購入時にこの金額が取得原価に調整される。

questions.subtopic: Derivative - Notional Amount vs Fair Value
questions.stem: Company A enters into an interest rate swap with a notional amount of $10,000,000 and a current fair value of $85,000. Under ASC 815, at what amount should the derivative be reported on the balance sheet?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0, since swaps are off-balance-sheet items

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-10-35-1, all derivatives must be recognized on the balance sheet at their fair value. The notional amount is not the amount recorded; it is merely the reference amount used to calculate cash flows. The fair value of $85,000 is the amount reported as either an asset or liability. Derivatives are never off-balance-sheet under ASC 815.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-10-35-1に基づき、全てのデリバティブは公正価値で貸借対照表に認識する。想定元本（$10,000,000）は記録される金額ではなく、キャッシュフロー計算の参照額に過ぎない。公正価値$85,000が資産または負債として報告される。ASC 815の下ではデリバティブはオフバランスにはならない。

questions.stem: A company purchases a put option (floor) with a strike price of $45 and simultaneously writes a call option (cap) with a strike price of $55 on the same commodity. This combined strategy is known as a:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Straddle

choices.label: B
choices.text: Collar

choices.label: C
choices.text: Butterfly spread

choices.label: D
choices.text: Calendar spread

questions.explanation: A collar is a strategy combining a purchased put (floor) with a written call (cap) on the same underlying. Under ASC 815-20-25-82, a collar can qualify as a hedging instrument if the net premium paid or received is not a net written option (i.e., the entity must not be a net writer of the combined option). The purchased put provides downside protection at $45, while the written call limits upside above $55.
questions.explanationJa: カラーは、同一原資産に対するプット（フロア）の購入とコール（キャップ）の売りを組み合わせた戦略である。ASC 815-20-25-82に基づき、受取プレミアムの純額がネット・ライトン・オプションとならない場合、ヘッジ手段として適格となり得る。プットが$45での下方保護を提供し、コールが$55以上の上昇を制限する。

questions.stem: Which of the following is a key accounting distinction between futures contracts and forward contracts under ASC 815?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Futures contracts are derivatives but forward contracts are not

choices.label: B
choices.text: Forward contracts must be settled in cash while futures may be physically settled

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both are accounted for as derivatives at fair value, but futures have daily margin settlements that affect cash flows

choices.label: D
choices.text: Futures contracts are always designated as hedges while forward contracts cannot be

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815, both futures and forward contracts meet the definition of derivatives and are measured at fair value on the balance sheet. The key operational distinction is that futures are exchange-traded with daily margin (mark-to-market) settlements, which creates daily cash flows. Forward contracts are privately negotiated OTC instruments without daily settlement. Both can be designated as hedging instruments if hedge accounting criteria are met.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815の下では、先物契約と先渡契約は共にデリバティブの定義を満たし、公正価値で測定される。主要な実務上の違いは、先物は取引所で取引され日次マージン（値洗い）決済があり日次キャッシュフローが生じる点である。先渡契約はOTCで私的に交渉され日次決済がない。両者ともヘッジ会計の要件を満たせばヘッジ手段に指定できる。

questions.stem: Under ASC 815, which of the following statements regarding a net written option is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A net written option can be designated as a hedging instrument if it is highly effective

choices.label: B
choices.text: A net written option cannot be designated as a hedging instrument because the potential loss exceeds the potential gain on the hedged item

choices.label: C
choices.text: A net written option is automatically excluded from derivative accounting

choices.label: D
choices.text: A net written option can only hedge firm commitments, not forecasted transactions

questions.explanation: ASC 815-20-25-94 prohibits a net written option from being designated as a hedging instrument. The rationale is that the premium received by the writer represents the maximum gain, while the potential loss is unlimited (or significantly exceeds the premium). This asymmetry means a written option cannot provide effective hedging because the potential loss on the hedging instrument exceeds the potential gain on the hedged item.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-20-25-94は、ネット・ライトン・オプション（正味売建オプション）をヘッジ手段として指定することを禁止している。売り手が受け取るプレミアムが最大利益となる一方、潜在的損失は無限（またはプレミアムを大幅に超える）であるため、ヘッジ手段としての潜在的損失がヘッジ対象の潜在的利益を超え、有効なヘッジを提供できない。

questions.stem: A company enters into a compound derivative that combines an interest rate swap and a currency forward. Under ASC 815, how should the company account for this compound derivative?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It must be bifurcated into separate derivatives for accounting purposes

choices.label: B
choices.text: It may be accounted for as a single derivative at fair value or separated into components, each of which may be designated separately as a hedging instrument

choices.label: C
choices.text: Compound derivatives are excluded from ASC 815 scope

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only the component with the larger notional amount is recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-20-25-78, a compound derivative may be viewed as a single combined instrument or may be separated into its component parts. Each component of a compound derivative can be separately designated as a hedging instrument if it independently qualifies. The entity has the choice in how to account for the compound derivative, but each component must meet all hedging criteria if designated.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-20-25-78に基づき、複合デリバティブは単一の合成商品として扱うことも、構成要素に分解することもできる。複合デリバティブの各構成要素は、個別に適格であれば別個にヘッジ手段として指定できる。会計処理の選択は企業に委ねられるが、指定する場合は各構成要素が全てのヘッジ要件を満たす必要がある。

questions.stem: A company voluntarily de-designates a cash flow hedge relationship while the forecasted transaction is still probable. Under ASC 815, how should the amounts in accumulated OCI related to the hedge be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Immediately reclassified to earnings upon de-designation

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remain in accumulated OCI and reclassified to earnings when the forecasted transaction affects earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reversed against the hedging instrument gain/loss account

choices.label: D
choices.text: Written off as a cumulative-effect adjustment to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-35-38, when a cash flow hedge is de-designated but the forecasted transaction remains probable, the net gain or loss in accumulated OCI at the time of de-designation continues to be reported in accumulated OCI. It is reclassified to earnings in the same period(s) in which the forecasted transaction affects earnings. Immediate reclassification to earnings is not appropriate unless the forecasted transaction is no longer probable.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-30-35-38に基づき、キャッシュフローヘッジを自発的に指定解除しても予定取引が依然として蓋然性がある場合、指定解除時のその他の包括利益累計額（AOCI）の純損益はAOCIに残留する。予定取引が損益に影響する期間に損益に振り替える。予定取引の蓋然性がなくなった場合にのみ即時損益振替が適切である。

questions.subtopic: Cash Flow Hedge - Discontinued Forecasted Transaction
questions.stem: A company designated a derivative as a cash flow hedge of a forecasted inventory purchase. After six months, it becomes probable that the forecasted transaction will not occur. At that point, accumulated OCI related to the hedge is a loss of $40,000. What is the journal entry to recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Loss $40,000, Credit Accumulated OCI $40,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Accumulated OCI $40,000, Credit Derivative Liability $40,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No entry is needed until the derivative expires

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $40,000, Credit Accumulated OCI $40,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-40-4, when it becomes probable that a hedged forecasted transaction will not occur, the net gain or loss that was accumulated in other comprehensive income shall be reclassified immediately into earnings. The $40,000 loss in accumulated OCI is reclassified: Debit Loss $40,000, Credit Accumulated OCI $40,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-30-40-4に基づき、ヘッジ対象の予定取引が発生しないことが蓋然的となった場合、その他の包括利益累計額に蓄積された純損益は直ちに損益に振り替える。AOCIの損失$40,000を振替：（借方）損失 $40,000、（貸方）その他の包括利益累計額 $40,000。

questions.stem: Under ASC 815, the shortcut method for assessing hedge effectiveness of an interest rate swap designated as a fair value hedge requires all of the following conditions EXCEPT:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The notional amount of the swap matches the principal of the hedged debt

choices.label: B
choices.text: The fair value of the swap is zero at inception

choices.label: C
choices.text: The swap must be exchange-traded rather than over-the-counter

choices.label: D
choices.text: The expiration date of the swap matches the maturity date of the hedged debt

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-20-25-104, the shortcut method requires: (1) the notional amount equals the principal, (2) the fair value of the swap is zero at inception, (3) the expiration of the swap matches the maturity of the debt, (4) there is no floor or cap, among other criteria. There is no requirement that the swap be exchange-traded. Interest rate swaps used in the shortcut method are typically OTC instruments.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-20-25-104に基づくショートカット法の要件：(1)想定元本が元本と一致、(2)スワップの公正価値が組成時にゼロ、(3)スワップの満期がヘッジ対象債務の満期と一致、(4)フロアやキャップがない等。取引所取引である要件はない。ショートカット法で使用される金利スワップは通常OTC商品である。

questions.stem: A company purchases an interest rate cap with a strike rate of 5% and a notional amount of $2,000,000. The reference rate (SOFR) is currently 6.5%. What is the payoff the company receives for the current period (assume annual settlement)?
questions.explanation: An interest rate cap pays the holder when the reference rate exceeds the strike rate. The payoff is calculated as: (Reference Rate - Strike Rate) x Notional Amount = (6.5% - 5.0%) x $2,000,000 = 1.5% x $2,000,000 = $30,000. The cap protects the holder against rising interest rates above the 5% level.
questions.explanationJa: 金利キャップは参照レートがストライクレートを超えた場合に保有者に支払われる。支払額 = (参照レート - ストライクレート) × 想定元本 = (6.5% - 5.0%) × $2,000,000 = 1.5% × $2,000,000 = $30,000。キャップは5%を超える金利上昇から保有者を保護する。

questions.stem: Company B enters into a commodity swap to pay a fixed price of $70 per barrel and receive the floating market price for 10,000 barrels of oil. At the end of the first settlement period, the market price is $76 per barrel. If Company B designates this as a cash flow hedge of a forecasted purchase, what amount is recorded in OCI?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $60,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $60,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $700,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $700,000

questions.explanation: Under the commodity swap, Company B receives the floating price ($76) and pays the fixed price ($70). The net settlement per barrel = $76 - $70 = $6. For 10,000 barrels: $6 x 10,000 = $60,000 gain. As a cash flow hedge under ASC 815-30-35-3, the effective portion of the gain on the hedging instrument is recorded in OCI. The gain of $60,000 is recorded in OCI.
questions.explanationJa: コモディティスワップでは、B社は変動価格（$76）を受け取り固定価格（$70）を支払う。1バレル当たりの純決済額 = $76 - $70 = $6。10,000バレルでは：$6 × 10,000 = $60,000の利益。ASC 815-30-35-3に基づくキャッシュフローヘッジとして、ヘッジ手段の利益の有効部分はOCIに計上される。

questions.stem: When measuring the fair value of a derivative under ASC 820, which of the following adjustments should be considered?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the credit risk of the counterparty (CVA)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the entity's own credit risk (DVA)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both the counterparty's credit risk (CVA) and the entity's own credit risk (DVA)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Credit risk adjustments are prohibited for derivatives under ASC 815

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820-10-35-17, fair value measurement must incorporate the risk of nonperformance, which for derivatives includes both the credit valuation adjustment (CVA) for counterparty credit risk and the debit valuation adjustment (DVA) for the entity's own credit risk. Both adjustments are necessary to arrive at the exit price that a market participant would use.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 820-10-35-17に基づき、公正価値測定には不履行リスクを組み込む必要があり、デリバティブの場合はカウンターパーティの信用リスクに対する信用価値調整（CVA）と自社の信用リスクに対する負債価値調整（DVA）の両方を含む。市場参加者が使用する出口価格に到達するためには両方の調整が必要である。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2017-12 (codified in ASC 815), which of the following is a change introduced for hedge accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Entities must assess hedge effectiveness using only the dollar-offset method

choices.label: B
choices.text: The requirement for retrospective hedge effectiveness assessment is eliminated, requiring only a qualitative prospective assessment after initial quantitative testing

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hedge accounting is eliminated for all fair value hedges

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cash flow hedge gains and losses must be recognized in earnings immediately rather than in OCI

questions.explanation: ASU 2017-12 introduced several targeted improvements to hedge accounting under ASC 815. A key change is the elimination of the requirement to separately measure and report hedge ineffectiveness through the income statement. Under the updated guidance, entities may elect to perform a qualitative (rather than quantitative) assessment of hedge effectiveness after initial quantitative testing, and retrospective effectiveness assessment is no longer required in subsequent periods.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-12はASC 815のヘッジ会計に複数の改善を導入した。主要な変更点は、ヘッジの非有効部分を損益計算書で個別に測定・報告する要件の廃止である。更新された指針では、当初の定量テスト後は定性的（定量的ではなく）な有効性評価を選択でき、後続期間での遡及的有効性評価は不要となった。

questions.stem: A company uses regression analysis to assess the effectiveness of a hedge relationship. Which of the following regression results would most clearly indicate the hedge is highly effective?

choices.label: A
choices.text: R-squared of 0.50, slope of -0.80

choices.label: B
choices.text: R-squared of 0.95, slope of -1.05

choices.label: C
choices.text: R-squared of 0.85, slope of -0.60

choices.label: D
choices.text: R-squared of 0.70, slope of -1.30

questions.explanation: Regression analysis assesses hedge effectiveness by examining the statistical relationship between changes in fair value of the hedging instrument and the hedged item. For high effectiveness: (1) R-squared should be at least 0.80 (indicating strong correlation), and (2) the slope should be between -0.80 and -1.25 (indicating the magnitudes of changes are closely matched). Option B with R-squared of 0.95 and slope of -1.05 clearly meets both criteria, indicating a highly effective hedge.
questions.explanationJa: 回帰分析はヘッジ手段とヘッジ対象の公正価値変動の統計的関係を分析しヘッジ有効性を評価する。高い有効性には：(1)R二乗が0.80以上（強い相関を示す）、(2)傾きが-0.80〜-1.25の範囲（変動の大きさが近接していることを示す）が必要。選択肢BのR二乗0.95、傾き-1.05は両基準を明確に満たし、高いヘッジ有効性を示す。

questions.subtopic: Cash Flow Hedge - Accumulated OCI Reclassification Pattern
questions.stem: A company has a cash flow hedge of a variable-rate borrowing using an interest rate swap. The swap has accumulated a gain of $120,000 in OCI over the term of the hedge. The underlying loan requires quarterly interest payments. How should the $120,000 OCI gain be reclassified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Entirely in the period the swap expires

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reclassified to earnings in each period that the hedged interest payments affect earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reclassified to the carrying amount of the debt

choices.label: D
choices.text: Remains in OCI permanently until the debt is repaid in full

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-35-38, amounts in accumulated OCI from a cash flow hedge are reclassified to earnings in the same period(s) during which the hedged forecasted transaction affects earnings. For a variable-rate borrowing, the hedged interest payments affect earnings each quarter when interest expense is recognized. Therefore, the OCI gain is reclassified proportionally as each hedged interest payment is recorded.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-30-35-38に基づき、キャッシュフローヘッジのAOCIの金額は、ヘッジ対象の予定取引が損益に影響する期間に損益に振り替える。変動金利借入の場合、ヘッジ対象の利息支払は各四半期に利息費用として認識される。したがって、OCIの利益はヘッジ対象の利息支払が記録される各期間に比例的に振り替えられる。

questions.subtopic: Fair Value Hedge - Portfolio Hedge of Interest Rate Risk
questions.stem: Under ASC 815, which of the following items may be hedged on a portfolio basis using a fair value hedge of interest rate risk?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A portfolio of equity securities

choices.label: B
choices.text: A portfolio of similar fixed-rate financial assets grouped by repricing periods

choices.label: C
choices.text: A portfolio of variable-rate debt instruments

choices.label: D
choices.text: A portfolio of forecasted sales transactions

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-20-25-12A (as amended by ASU 2017-12), entities may designate a portfolio of similar fixed-rate financial assets (or liabilities) grouped by repricing date as the hedged item in a fair value hedge of interest rate risk. This is sometimes called the portfolio layer method. Variable-rate instruments are not appropriate for fair value hedges of interest rate risk (they suit cash flow hedges), and equity securities and forecasted transactions do not qualify for portfolio fair value hedges.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-20-25-12A（ASU 2017-12改正後）に基づき、金利改定日ごとにグループ化された類似の固定金利金融資産（または負債）のポートフォリオを、金利リスクの公正価値ヘッジのヘッジ対象として指定できる。変動金利商品は公正価値ヘッジではなくキャッシュフローヘッジに適し、株式証券や予定取引はポートフォリオ公正価値ヘッジの対象とならない。

questions.stem: A company enters into a derivative contract and determines at inception that the model-based fair value is $15,000 (an asset), but no observable market data exists to validate the model inputs. Under ASC 820, how should the $15,000 Day 1 gain be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognized immediately in earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized in OCI until the derivative is settled

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred and recognized over the life of the derivative or when inputs become observable

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recorded as a reduction of the derivative asset

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820-10-35-16D, when the fair value of a derivative at inception differs from the transaction price and the measurement relies on significant unobservable inputs (Level 3), the Day 1 gain or loss is deferred. The deferred amount is recognized in earnings over the life of the instrument or when the valuation inputs become observable, or upon settlement. Immediate recognition is only appropriate when fair value is determined using Level 1 or Level 2 inputs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 820-10-35-16Dに基づき、デリバティブの組成時の公正価値が取引価格と異なり、その測定が重要な観察不能インプット（レベル3）に依拠する場合、Day 1損益は繰延べられる。繰延額は商品の存続期間にわたって、評価インプットが観察可能になった時点、または決済時に損益に認識される。即時認識はレベル1またはレベル2インプットで公正価値が決定される場合にのみ適切である。

questions.stem: A ski resort enters into a weather derivative that pays $50,000 for every inch of snowfall below 40 inches during the season. Under ASC 815, how should this instrument be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a derivative measured at fair value under ASC 815

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a weather derivative excluded from ASC 815 scope and accounted for under ASC 815-45

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an insurance contract under ASC 944

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a guarantee under ASC 460

questions.explanation: ASC 815-10-15-59A provides a scope exception for weather derivatives. These instruments have underlyings based on climatic or geological variables (e.g., snowfall, temperature, rainfall) and are excluded from ASC 815 general derivative accounting. Instead, they are accounted for under ASC 815-45, which addresses weather derivatives specifically. The instrument is not an insurance contract because it does not require a loss to trigger payment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-10-15-59Aは天候デリバティブに適用除外を設けている。気候・地質変数（降雪量、気温、降雨量等）を基礎数値とするこれらの商品は、ASC 815の一般的なデリバティブ会計から除外される。代わりにASC 815-45（天候デリバティブ専用）に基づいて処理する。支払いのトリガーに損失が不要であるため、保険契約ではない。

questions.stem: A company pays a premium of $45,000 to acquire a swaption (option to enter into an interest rate swap). The swaption expires unexercised. What is the total impact on earnings from the swaption?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $45,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $45,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss equal to the fair value of the swap that was not entered into

questions.explanation: A swaption is a derivative under ASC 815 and is recorded at fair value. At inception, the premium paid of $45,000 is the fair value of the swaption asset. As the swaption is marked to market each period, changes in fair value are recognized in earnings (assuming no hedge designation). When the swaption expires unexercised, its fair value is zero. The cumulative change in fair value from $45,000 to $0 results in a total loss of $45,000 recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: スワプションはASC 815に基づくデリバティブであり、公正価値で記録する。組成時の支払プレミアム$45,000がスワプション資産の公正価値となる。各期に公正価値で時価評価し変動を損益に認識する（ヘッジ指定なしの場合）。スワプションが未行使で満期を迎えると公正価値はゼロとなり、$45,000からゼロへの公正価値の累積変動が損失$45,000として損益に認識される。

questions.stem: A company posts $500,000 in cash collateral (initial margin) for a centrally cleared interest rate swap with a fair value of $120,000 (asset). How should the cash collateral and derivative be reported on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Derivative asset of $620,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Derivative asset of $120,000 and a separate receivable of $500,000 for the margin deposit

choices.label: C
choices.text: Cash of $500,000 and derivative asset of $120,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Derivative asset of $500,000 only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-10-45, cash collateral posted as margin is not part of the derivative's fair value. The derivative is reported at its fair value of $120,000 as an asset. The $500,000 cash margin posted is reported as a separate receivable (deposit or margin account), as the entity retains the right to receive the cash back. The two amounts are not combined into a single balance sheet line item.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-10-45に基づき、マージンとして差し入れた現金担保はデリバティブの公正価値の一部ではない。デリバティブは公正価値$120,000で資産として報告する。差し入れたマージン$500,000は現金の返還請求権があるため、別個の受取債権（預託金・マージン勘定）として報告する。2つの金額を単一の貸借対照表項目に合算しない。

questions.stem: Company C enters into a total return swap where it receives the total return on a bond portfolio (interest + price changes) and pays SOFR + 50 basis points on a notional of $5,000,000. During the first quarter, the bond portfolio generates interest of $62,500 and appreciates by $35,000. SOFR averages 4.0% for the quarter. What is the net settlement for Company C?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net receipt of $41,250

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net payment of $41,250

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net receipt of $97,500

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net receipt of $35,000

questions.explanation: Company C receives total return: Interest $62,500 + Appreciation $35,000 = $97,500. Company C pays: (SOFR + 0.50%) x Notional x (1/4 year) = (4.0% + 0.50%) x $5,000,000 x 0.25 = 4.50% x $5,000,000 x 0.25 = $56,250. Net settlement = $97,500 - $56,250 = $41,250 net receipt for Company C.

questions.stem: A company issues a $1,000,000 bond that pays interest based on the S&P 500 index return rather than a fixed or variable interest rate. Under ASC 815, how should the issuer account for this hybrid instrument?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Account for the entire instrument at amortized cost as a single bond

choices.label: B
choices.text: Bifurcate the embedded equity-linked derivative from the host debt contract and account for each separately

choices.label: C
choices.text: Account for the entire instrument at fair value as a single derivative

choices.label: D
choices.text: Classify the entire instrument as an equity instrument

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-15-25-1, an embedded derivative must be bifurcated from the host contract when: (1) the economic characteristics and risks of the embedded derivative are not clearly and closely related to the host contract, (2) a separate instrument with the same terms would be a derivative, and (3) the hybrid instrument is not measured at fair value with changes in earnings. An equity-linked return on a debt host is not clearly and closely related to the debt host, so bifurcation is required. The debt host is accounted for at amortized cost and the embedded equity derivative at fair value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-15-25-1に基づき、組込デリバティブは以下の場合にホスト契約から分離する：(1)組込デリバティブの経済的特性・リスクがホスト契約と明確かつ密接に関連していない、(2)同条件の独立商品がデリバティブとなる、(3)混合商品が公正価値で測定されていない。債務ホストに対する株式連動リターンは明確かつ密接に関連しないため、分離が必要。債務ホストは償却原価、組込株式デリバティブは公正価値で処理する。

questions.stem: Company D sells protection through a credit default swap (CDS) on a $3,000,000 reference entity. Company D receives an annual premium of 200 basis points. After one year, a credit event occurs and the recovery rate is 40%. What is the net loss for Company D for the year?
questions.explanation: Company D (protection seller) receives annual premium = 200 bps x $3,000,000 = 2.00% x $3,000,000 = $60,000. Upon the credit event, Company D must pay the loss given default: Notional x (1 - Recovery Rate) = $3,000,000 x (1 - 0.40) = $3,000,000 x 0.60 = $1,800,000. Net loss for the year = Payment made - Premium received = $1,800,000 - $60,000 = $1,740,000.
